26_1_28.NLT: All around him was a glowing halo, like a rainbow shining in the clouds on a rainy day. This is what the glory of the LORD looked like to me. When I saw it, I fell face down on the ground, and I heard someone’s voice speaking to me.
26_1_28.WEB: As the appearance of the rainbow that is in the cloud in the day of rain, so was the appearance of the brightness all around. This was the appearance of the likeness of Yahweh’s glory. When I saw it, I fell on my face, and I heard a voice of one that spoke.
26_1_28.CSB17: The appearance of the brilliant light all around was like that of a rainbow in a cloud on a rainy day. <sup>ⓛ</sup> This was the appearance of the likeness of the LORD’s glory. <sup>ⓜ</sup> When I saw it, I fell facedown and heard a voice speaking. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
26_1_28.AMP: As the appearance of the rainbow in the clouds on a rainy day, so was the appearance of the surrounding radiance. This was the appearance of the likeness of the glory and brilliance of the LORD. And when I saw it, I fell face downward and I heard a voice of One speaking.
26_1_28.MSG:
The way a rainbow springs out of the sky on a rainy day—that's what it was like. It turned out to be the Glory of God!
When I saw all this, I fell to my knees, my face to the ground. Then I heard a voice.
26_1_28.NIV:
Like the appearance of a rainbow in the clouds on a rainy day, so was the radiance around him.
This was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the Lord. When I saw it, I fell facedown, and I heard the voice of one speaking.
26_1_28.NRSVCE: Like the bow in a cloud on a rainy day, such was the appearance of the splendor all around. This was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the LORD. When I saw it, I fell on my face, and I heard the voice of someone speaking.

26_3_18.CSB17: If I say to the wicked person, <sup>ⓝ</sup> ‘You will surely die,’ but you do not warn him — you don’t speak out to warn him about his wicked way in order to save his life — that wicked person will die for his iniquity. Yet I will hold you responsible for his blood.
26_3_18.AMP: When I say to the wicked, 'You will certainly die,' and you do not warn him or speak out to tell him to turn from his wicked way to save his life, that same evil man will die in his sin, but you will be responsible for his blood.
26_3_18.MSG: If I say to the wicked, 'You are going to die,' and you don't sound the alarm warning them that it's a matter of life or death, they will die and it will be your fault. I'll hold you responsible.
26_3_18.NIV: When I say to a wicked man, ‘You will surely die,’ and you do not warn him or speak out to dissuade him from his evil ways in order to save his life, that wicked man will die for his sin, and I will hold you accountable for his blood.
26_3_18.NRSVCE: If I say to the wicked, “You shall surely die,” and you give them no warning, or speak to warn the wicked from their wicked way, in order to save their life, those wicked persons shall die for their iniquity; but their blood I will require at your hand.

26_3_19.NKJV: Yet, if you warn the wicked, and he does not turn from his wickedness, nor from his wicked way, he shall die in his iniquity; but you have delivered your soul.
26_3_19.KJV: Yet if thou warn<S>2094</S> the wicked<S>7563</S>, and he turn<S>7725</S> not from his wickedness<S>7562</S>, nor from his wicked<S>7563</S> way<S>1870</S>, he shall die<S>4191</S> in his iniquity<S>5771</S>; but thou hast delivered<S>5337</S> thy soul<S>5315</S>.
26_3_19.ESV: But if you warn the wicked, and he does not turn from his wickedness, or from his wicked way, he shall die for his iniquity, but you will have delivered your soul.
26_3_19.NASB: 'Yet if you have warned the wicked and he does not turn from his wickedness or from his wicked way, he shall die in his iniquity; but you have delivered yourself.
26_3_19.NLT: If you warn them and they refuse to repent and keep on sinning, they will die in their sins. But you will have saved yourself because you obeyed me.

26_3_19.AMP: However, if you have warned the wicked and he does not turn from his wickedness or from his wicked way, he will die in his sin; but you have freed yourself [from responsibility].
26_3_19.MSG: But if you warn the wicked and they keep right on sinning anyway, they'll most certainly die for their sin, but you won't die. You'll have saved your life.
26_3_19.NIV: But if you do warn the wicked man and he does not turn from his wickedness or from his evil ways, he will die for his sin; but you will have saved yourself.
26_3_19.NRSVCE: But if you warn the wicked, and they do not turn from their wickedness, or from their wicked way, they shall die for their iniquity; but you will have saved your life.

26_3_20.NKJV: “Again, when a righteous man turns from his righteousness and commits iniquity, and I lay a stumbling block before him, he shall die; because you did not give him warning, he shall die in his sin, and his righteousness which he has done shall not be remembered; but his blood I will require at your hand.
26_3_20.KJV: Again<S>7725</S>, When a righteous<S>6662</S> man doth turn<S>7725</S> from his righteousness<S>6664</S>, and commit<S>6213</S> iniquity<S>5766</S>, and I lay<S>5414</S> a stumblingblock<S>4383</S> before<S>6440</S> him, he shall die<S>4191</S>: because thou hast not given him warning<S>2094</S>, he shall die<S>4191</S> in his sin<S>2403</S>, and his righteousness<S>6666</S> which he hath done<S>6213</S> shall not be remembered<S>2142</S>; but his blood<S>1818</S> will I require<S>1245</S> at thine hand<S>3027</S>. <sup>righteousness which: Heb. righteousnesses</sup>

26_3_20.NASB: 'Again, when a righteous man turns away from his righteousness and commits iniquity, and I place an obstacle before him, he will die; since you have not warned him, he shall die in his sin, and his righteous deeds which he has done shall not be remembered; but his blood I will require at your hand.

Yet I will hold you responsible for his blood.

26_3_20.NIV: “Again, when a righteous man turns from his righteousness and does evil, and I put a stumbling block before him, he will die. Since you did not warn him, he will die for his sin. The righteous things he did will not be remembered, and I will hold you accountable for his blood.

26_3_21.AMP: However, if you have warned the righteous man not to sin and he does not sin, he will surely live because he took warning; also you have freed yourself [from responsibility]."

26_4_3.CSB17: Take an iron plate and set it up as an iron wall between yourself and the city. Face it so that it is under siege, and besiege it. This will be a sign for the house of Israel. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
26_4_3.AMP: Further, take an iron plate and place it as an iron wall between you and the city, and set your face toward it so that it is under siege, and besiege it. This is a sign to the house of Israel.
26_4_3.MSG: Then get an iron skillet and place it upright between you and the city—an iron wall. Face the model: The city shall be under siege and you shall be the besieger. This is a sign to the family of Israel.
26_4_3.NIV: Then take an iron pan, place it as an iron wall between you and the city and turn your face toward it. It will be under siege, and you shall besiege it. This will be a sign to the house of Israel.
26_4_3.NRSVCE: Then take an iron plate and place it as an iron wall between you and the city; set your face toward it, and let it be in a state of siege, and press the siege against it. This is a sign for the house of Israel.

26_4_4.NKJV: “Lie also on your left side, and lay the iniquity of the house of Israel upon it. According to the number of the days that you lie on it, you shall bear their iniquity.

26_4_7.NKJV: “Therefore you shall set your face toward the siege of Jerusalem; your arm shall be uncovered, and you shall prophesy against it.

26_4_12.NKJV: And you shall eat it as barley cakes; and bake it using fuel of human waste in their sight.”

26_4_14.NKJV: So I said, “Ah, Lord GOD! Indeed I have never defiled myself from my youth till now; I have never eaten what died of itself or was torn by beasts, nor has abominable flesh ever come into my mouth.”

26_4_14.ESV: Then I said, “Ah, Lord GOD! Behold, I have never defiled myself.  From my youth up till now I have never eaten what died of itself or was torn by beasts, nor has tainted meat come into my mouth.”
26_4_14.NASB: But I said, 'Ah, Lord GOD! Behold, I have never been defiled; for from my youth until now I have never eaten what died of itself or was torn by beasts, nor has any unclean meat ever entered my mouth.'

So they went out and struck the city.

26_10_11.NKJV: When they went, they went toward any of their four directions; they did not turn aside when they went, but followed in the direction the head was facing. They did not turn aside when they went.

26_10_11.NASB: When they moved, they went in [any of] their four directions without turning as they went; but they followed in the direction which they faced, without turning as they went.

I threw you, disgraced, off the mountain of God.
I threw you out—you, the anointed angel-cherub.

26_32_27.NKJV: They do not lie with the mighty Who are fallen of the uncircumcised, Who have gone down to hell with their weapons of war; They have laid their swords under their heads, But their iniquities will be on their bones, Because of the terror of the mighty in the land of the living.

The righteous person won’t be able to survive by his righteousness on the day he sins.

26_33_26.AMP: You rely on your sword [as your security]; you commit outrageous and disgraceful acts and each of you defiles his neighbor's wife. Should you then take possession of the land?"'
26_33_26.MSG: You rely on the sword, you engage in obscenities, you indulge in sex at random—anyone, anytime. And you still expect to own this land?'

26_33_27.NKJV: “Say thus to them, ‘Thus says the Lord GOD: “As I live, surely those who are in the ruins shall fall by the sword, and the one who is in the open field I will give to the beasts to be devoured, and those who are in the strongholds and caves shall die of the pestilence.

26_33_30.NKJV: “As for you, son of man, the children of your people are talking about you beside the walls and in the doors of the houses; and they speak to one another, everyone saying to his brother, ‘Please come and hear what the word is that comes from the LORD.’

26_34_4.NKJV: The weak you have not strengthened, nor have you healed those who were sick, nor bound up the broken, nor brought back what was driven away, nor sought what was lost; but with force and cruelty you have ruled them.
26_34_4.KJV: The diseased<S>2470</S> have ye not strengthened<S>2388</S>, neither have ye healed<S>7495</S> that which was sick<S>2470</S>, neither have ye bound up<S>2280</S> that which was broken<S>7665</S>, neither have ye brought again<S>7725</S> that which was driven away<S>5080</S>, neither have ye sought<S>1245</S> that which was lost<S>6</S>; but with force<S>2394</S> and with cruelty<S>6531</S> have ye ruled<S>7287</S> them.
26_34_4.ESV: The weak you have not strengthened, the sick you have not healed, the injured you have not bound up, the strayed you have not brought back, the lost you have not sought, and with force and harshness you have ruled them.
26_34_4.NASB: 'Those who are sickly you have not strengthened, the diseased you have not healed, the broken you have not bound up, the scattered you have not brought back, nor have you sought for the lost; but with force and with severity you have dominated them.
26_34_4.NLT: You have not taken care of the weak. You have not tended the sick or bound up the injured. You have not gone looking for those who have wandered away and are lost. Instead, you have ruled them with harshness and cruelty.
26_34_4.WEB: You haven’t strengthened the diseased, neither have you healed that which was sick, neither have you bound up that which was broken, neither have you brought back that which was driven away, neither have you sought that which was lost; but with force and with rigor you have ruled over them.

26_34_4.MSG: You don't build up the weak ones, don't heal the sick, don't doctor the injured, don't go after the strays, don't look for the lost. You bully and badger them.

26_34_4.NRSVCE: You have not strengthened the weak, you have not healed the sick, you have not bound up the injured, you have not brought back the strayed, you have not sought the lost, but with force and harshness you have ruled them.

26_34_18.NLT: Isn’t it enough for you to keep the best of the pastures for yourselves? Must you also trample down the rest? Isn’t it enough for you to drink clear water for yourselves? Must you also muddy the rest with your feet?
26_34_18.WEB: Does it seem a small thing to you to have fed on the good pasture, but you must tread down with your feet the residue of your pasture? and to have drunk of the clear waters, but you must foul the residue with your feet?
26_34_18.CSB17: Isn’t it enough for you to feed on the good pasture? Must you also trample the rest of the pasture with your feet? Or isn’t it enough that you drink the clear water? Must you also muddy the rest with your feet?
26_34_18.AMP: Is it too little a thing for you that you [unrighteous ones who are well-fed] feed in the best pasture, yet you must trample down with your feet [of wickedness] the rest of your pastures? Or that you drink clear [still] water, yet you must muddy with your feet [of wickedness] the rest [of the water]?
26_34_18.MSG: Aren't you satisfied to feed in good pasture without taking over the whole place? Can't you be satisfied to drink from the clear stream without muddying the water with your feet?
26_34_18.NIV: Is it not enough for you to feed on the good pasture? Must you also trample the rest of your pasture with your feet? Is it not enough for you to drink clear water? Must you also muddy the rest with your feet?
26_34_18.NRSVCE: Is it not enough for you to feed on the good pasture, but you must tread down with your feet the rest of your pasture? When you drink of clear water, must you foul the rest with your feet?

The Valley of Dry Bones
The hand of the Lord was upon me, and he brought me out by the Spirit of the Lord and set me in the middle of a valley; it was full of bones.
26_37_1.NRSVCE: The hand of the LORD came upon me, and he brought me out by the spirit of the LORD and set me down in the middle of a valley; it was full of bones.

26_37_2.NKJV: Then He caused me to pass by them all around, and behold, there were very many in the open valley; and indeed they were very dry.

26_37_2.NLT: He led me all around among the bones that covered the valley floor. They were scattered everywhere across the ground and were completely dried out.

He said to me, "Son of man, can these bones live?"

26_39_13.NLT: Everyone in Israel will help, for it will be a glorious victory for Israel when I demonstrate my glory on that day, says the Sovereign LORD.
26_39_13.WEB: Yes, all the people of the land shall bury them; and it shall be to them a renown in the day that I shall be glorified, says the Lord Yahweh.

26_40_8.CSB17: Next he measured the gate’s portico;
26_40_8.AMP: He also measured the porch of the gate facing inward [toward the temple of the LORD], one rod.

26_40_9.NASB: He measured the porch of the gate, eight cubits; and its side pillars, two cubits. And the porch of the gate was faced inward.
26_40_9.NLT: It was 14 feet across, with supporting columns 3 1⁄2 feet thick. This entry room was at the inner end of the gateway structure, facing toward the Temple.
26_40_9.WEB: Then measured he the porch of the gate, eight cubits; and its posts, two cubits; and the porch of the gate was toward the house.

26_40_9.MSG: twelve feet deep, flanked by pillars three feet thick. The porch opened onto the Temple courtyard.
26_40_9.NIV: it was eight cubits deep and its jambs were two cubits thick. The portico of the gateway faced the temple.

26_40_10.NKJV: In the eastern gateway were three gate chambers on one side and three on the other; the three were all the same size; also the gateposts were of the same size on this side and that side.

26_40_10.ESV: And there were three side rooms on either side of the east gate. The three were of the same size, and the jambs on either side were of the same size.
26_40_10.NASB: The guardrooms of the gate toward the east [numbered] three on each side; the three of them had the same measurement. The side pillars also had the same measurement on each side.
26_40_10.NLT: There were three guard alcoves on each side of the gateway passage. Each had the same measurements, and the dividing walls separating them were also identical.
26_40_10.WEB: The lodges of the gate eastward were three on this side, and three on that side; they three were of one measure: and the posts had one measure on this side and on that side.

26_40_10.AMP: There were three guardrooms on each side of the gate toward the east; the three were the same size, and the side pillars on each side measured the same.

26_40_11.NKJV: He measured the width of the entrance to the gateway, ten cubits; and the length of the gate, thirteen cubits.
26_40_11.KJV: And he measured<S>4058</S> the breadth<S>7341</S> of the entry<S>6607</S> of the gate<S>8179</S>, ten<S>6235</S> cubits<S>520</S>; and the length<S>753</S> of the gate<S>8179</S>, thirteen<S>7969</S> <S>6240</S> cubits<S>520</S>.
26_40_11.ESV: Then he measured the width of the opening of the gateway, ten cubits; and the length of the gateway, thirteen cubits.
26_40_11.NASB: And he measured the width of the gateway, ten cubits, and the length of the gate, thirteen cubits.
26_40_11.NLT: The man measured the gateway entrance, which was 17 1⁄2 feet wide at the opening and 22 3⁄4 feet wide in the gateway passage.
26_40_11.WEB: He measured the width of the opening of the gate, ten cubits; and the length of the gate, thirteen cubits;
26_40_11.CSB17: Then he measured the width of the gate’s entrance; it was 17½ feet, <sup>[8]</sup> while the width <sup>[9]</sup> of the gate was 22¾ feet. <sup>[10]</sup>

26_40_12.ESV: There was a barrier before the side rooms, one cubit on either side. And the side rooms were six cubits on either side.
26_40_12.NASB: [There was] a barrier [wall] one cubit [wide] in front of the guardrooms on each side; and the guardrooms [were] six cubits [square] on each side.
26_40_12.NLT: In front of each of the guard alcoves was a 21-inch curb. The alcoves themselves were 10 1⁄2 feet on each side.
26_40_12.WEB: and a border before the lodges, one cubit on this side, and a border, one cubit on that side; and the lodges, six cubits on this side, and six cubits on that side.
26_40_12.CSB17: There was a barrier of 21 inches <sup>[11]</sup> in front of the recesses on both sides, and the recesses on each side were 10½ feet <sup>[12]</sup> square.

26_40_13.NKJV: Then he measured the gateway from the roof of one gate chamber to the roof of the other; the width was twenty-five cubits, as door faces door.
26_40_13.KJV: He measured<S>4058</S> then the gate<S>8179</S> from the roof<S>1406</S> of one little chamber<S>8372</S> to the roof<S>1406</S> of another: the breadth<S>7341</S> was five<S>2568</S> and twenty<S>6242</S> cubits<S>520</S>, door<S>6607</S> against door<S>6607</S>.

26_40_14.NKJV: He measured the gateposts, sixty cubits high, and the court all around the gateway extended to the gatepost.

26_40_14.ESV: He measured also the vestibule, sixty cubits. And around the vestibule of the gateway was the court.
26_40_14.NASB: He made the side pillars sixty cubits [high]; the gate [extended] round about to the side pillar of the courtyard.
26_40_14.NLT: He measured the dividing walls all along the inside of the gateway up to the entry room of the gateway; this distance was 105 feet.
26_40_14.WEB: He made also posts, sixty cubits; and the court reached to the posts, around the gate.

26_40_15.ESV: From the front of the gate at the entrance to the front of the inner vestibule of the gate was fifty cubits.
26_40_15.NASB: [From] the front of the entrance gate to the front of the inner porch of the gate [was] fifty cubits.
26_40_15.NLT: The full length of the gateway passage was 87 1⁄2 feet from one end to the other.
26_40_15.WEB: From the forefront of the gate at the entrance to the forefront of the inner porch of the gate were fifty cubits.

26_40_15.NRSVCE: From the front of the gate at the entrance to the end of the inner vestibule of the gate was fifty cubits.

26_40_16.NKJV: There were beveled window frames in the gate chambers and in their intervening archways on the inside of the gateway all around, and likewise in the vestibules. There were windows all around on the inside. And on each gatepost were palm trees.
26_40_16.KJV: And there were narrow<S>331</S> windows<S>2474</S> to the little chambers<S>8372</S>, and to their posts<S>352</S> within<S>6441</S> the gate<S>8179</S> round about<S>5439</S>, and likewise to the arches<S>361</S>: and windows<S>2474</S> were round about<S>5439</S> inward<S>6441</S>: and upon each post<S>352</S> were palm trees<S>8561</S>. <sup>narrow: Heb. closed</sup> <sup>arches: or, galleries, or, porches</sup> <sup>inward: or, within</sup>
26_40_16.ESV: And the gateway had windows all around, narrowing inwards toward the side rooms and toward their jambs, and likewise the vestibule had windows all around inside, and on the jambs were palm trees.
26_40_16.NASB: [There were] shuttered windows [looking] toward the guardrooms, and toward their side pillars within the gate all around, and likewise for the porches. And [there were] windows all around inside; and on [each] side pillar [were] palm tree ornaments.
26_40_16.NLT: There were recessed windows that narrowed inward through the walls of the guard alcoves and their dividing walls. There were also windows in the entry room. The surfaces of the dividing walls were decorated with carved palm trees.
26_40_16.WEB: There were closed windows to the lodges, and to their posts within the gate all around, and likewise to the arches; and windows were around inward; and on each post were palm trees.
26_40_16.CSB17: The recesses and their jambs had beveled windows all around the inside of the gate. <sup>ⓚ</sup> The porticoes also had windows all around on the inside. Each jamb was decorated with palm trees. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
26_40_16.AMP: There were shuttered windows looking toward the guardrooms, and toward their side pillars within the gate all around, and likewise for the porches. And windows were all around inside; and palm tree decorations were on each side pillar.

26_40_16.NRSVCE: The recesses and their pilasters had windows, with shutters on the inside of the gateway all around, and the vestibules also had windows on the inside all around; and on the pilasters were palm trees.

26_40_17.NKJV: Then he brought me into the outer court; and there were chambers and a pavement made all around the court; thirty chambers faced the pavement.

26_40_17.ESV: Then he brought me into the outer court. And behold, there were chambers and a pavement, all around the court. Thirty chambers faced the pavement.
26_40_17.NASB: Then he brought me into the outer court, and behold, [there were] chambers and a pavement made for the court all around; thirty chambers faced the pavement.

26_40_17.WEB: Then brought he me into the outer court; and behold, there were rooms and a pavement, made for the court all around: thirty rooms were on the pavement.

The Outer Court

26_40_18.NKJV: The pavement was by the side of the gateways, corresponding to the length of the gateways; this was the lower pavement.
26_40_18.KJV: And the pavement<S>7531</S> by the side<S>3802</S> of the gates<S>8179</S> over against<S>5980</S> the length<S>753</S> of the gates<S>8179</S> was the lower<S>8481</S> pavement<S>7531</S>.

26_40_18.NLT: This pavement flanked the gates and extended out from the walls into the courtyard the same distance as the gateway entrance. This was the lower pavement.

26_40_18.CSB17: which flanked the courtyard’s gates and corresponded to the length of the gates; this was the lower pavement.

26_40_19.WEB: Then he measured the width from the forefront of the lower gate to the forefront of the inner court outside, one hundred cubits, both on the east and on the north.

He measured the distance from the front of the entrance gateway across to the entrance of the inner court: one hundred fifty feet.
Then he took me to the north side.

26_40_20.NKJV: On the outer court was also a gateway facing north, and he measured its length and its width.
26_40_20.KJV: And the gate<S>8179</S> of the outward<S>2435</S> court<S>2691</S> that looked<S>6440</S> toward<S>1870</S> the north<S>6828</S>, he measured<S>4058</S> the length<S>753</S> thereof, and the breadth<S>7341</S> thereof. <sup>that looked: Heb. whose face was</sup>
26_40_20.ESV: As for the gate that faced toward the north, belonging to the outer court, he measured its length and its breadth.
26_40_20.NASB: [As for] the gate of the outer court which faced the north, he measured its length and its width.
26_40_20.NLT: The man measured the gateway on the north just like the one on the east.

26_40_20.AMP: And as for the gate of the outer courtyard which faced the north he measured [both] its length and its width.

The North Gate

26_40_21.NKJV: Its gate chambers, three on this side and three on that side, its gateposts and its archways, had the same measurements as the first gate; its length was fifty cubits and its width twenty-five cubits.
26_40_21.KJV: And the little chambers<S>8372</S> thereof were three<S>7969</S> on this side and three<S>7969</S> on that side; and the posts<S>352</S> thereof and the arches<S>361</S> thereof were after the measure<S>4060</S> of the first<S>7223</S> gate<S>8179</S>: the length<S>753</S> thereof was fifty<S>2572</S> cubits<S>520</S>, and the breadth<S>7341</S> five<S>2568</S> and twenty<S>6242</S> cubits<S>520</S>. <sup>arches: or, galleries, or, porches</sup>
26_40_21.ESV: Its side rooms, three on either side, and its jambs and its vestibule were of the same size as those of the first gate. Its length was fifty cubits, and its breadth twenty-five cubits.
26_40_21.NASB: It had three guardrooms on each side; and its side pillars and its porches had the same measurement as the first gate. Its length [was] fifty cubits and the width twenty-five cubits.
26_40_21.NLT: Here, too, there were three guard alcoves on each side, with dividing walls and an entry room. All the measurements matched those of the east gateway. The gateway passage was 87 1⁄2 feet long and 43 3⁄4 feet wide between the back walls of facing guard alcoves.
26_40_21.WEB: The lodges of it were three on this side and three on that side; and its posts and its arches were after the measure of the first gate: its length was fifty cubits, and the width twenty-five cubits.
26_40_21.CSB17: Its three recesses on each side, its jambs, and its portico had the same measurements as the first gate: 87½ feet long and 43¾ feet wide.
26_40_21.AMP: Its guardrooms, three on each side, and its side pillars and its porches had the same measurement as the first gate. Its length was fifty cubits and the width was twenty-five cubits.

26_40_22.ESV: And its windows, its vestibule, and its palm trees were of the same size as those of the gate that faced toward the east. And by seven steps people would go up to it, and find its vestibule before them.
26_40_22.NASB: Its windows and its porches and its palm tree ornaments [had] the same measurements as the gate which faced toward the east; and it was reached by seven steps, and its porch [was] in front of them.
26_40_22.NLT: The windows, the entry room, and the palm tree decorations were identical to those in the east gateway. There were seven steps leading up to the gateway entrance, and the entry room was at the inner end of the gateway passage.
26_40_22.WEB: The windows of it, and its arches, and the palm trees of it, were after the measure of the gate whose prospect is toward the east; and they went up to it by seven steps; and its arches were before them.
26_40_22.CSB17: Its windows, portico, and palm trees had the same measurements as those of the gate that faced east. Seven steps led up to the gate, and its portico was ahead of them.
26_40_22.AMP: Its windows and its porches and its palm tree decorations had the same measurements as the gate that faced toward the east. It was reached by going up seven steps, and its porch was in front of them.
26_40_22.MSG: The windows and palm trees were identical to the east gateway. Seven steps led up to it, and its porch faced inward.
26_40_22.NIV: Its openings, its portico and its palm tree decorations had the same measurements as those of the gate facing east. Seven steps led up to it, with its portico opposite them.
26_40_22.NRSVCE: Its windows, its vestibule, and its palm trees were of the same size as those of the gate that faced toward the east. Seven steps led up to it; and its vestibule was on the inside.

26_40_23.NKJV: A gate of the inner court was opposite the northern gateway, just as the eastern gateway; and he measured from gateway to gateway, one hundred cubits.
26_40_23.KJV: And the gate<S>8179</S> of the inner<S>6442</S> court<S>2691</S> was over against the gate<S>8179</S> toward the north<S>6828</S>, and toward the east<S>6921</S>; and he measured<S>4058</S> from gate<S>8179</S> to gate<S>8179</S> an hundred<S>3967</S> cubits<S>520</S>.
26_40_23.ESV: And opposite the gate on the north, as on the east, was a gate to the inner court. And he measured from gate to gate, a hundred cubits.
26_40_23.NASB: The inner court had a gate opposite the gate on the north as well as [the gate] on the east; and he measured a hundred cubits from gate to gate.
26_40_23.NLT: Here on the north side, just as on the east, there was another gateway leading to the Temple’s inner courtyard directly opposite this outer gateway. The distance between the two gateways was 175 feet.
26_40_23.WEB: There was a gate to the inner court facing the other gate, on the north and on the east; and he measured from gate to gate one hundred cubits.
26_40_23.CSB17: The inner court had a gate facing the north gate, like the one on the east. He measured the distance from gate to gate; it was 175 feet.
26_40_23.AMP: The inner courtyard had a gate opposite the gate on the north as well as the gate on the east; and he measured a hundred cubits from gate to gate.
26_40_23.MSG: Opposite this gate complex was a gate complex to the inside courtyard, on the north as on the east. The distance between the two was one hundred seventy-five feet.
26_40_23.NIV: There was a gate to the inner court facing the north gate, just as there was on the east. He measured from one gate to the opposite one; it was a hundred cubits.
26_40_23.NRSVCE: Opposite the gate on the north, as on the east, was a gate to the inner court; he measured from gate to gate, one hundred cubits.

26_40_24.NKJV: After that he brought me toward the south, and there a gateway was facing south; and he measured its gateposts and archways according to these same measurements.
26_40_24.KJV: After that he brought<S>3212</S> me toward<S>1870</S> the south<S>1864</S>, and behold a gate<S>8179</S> toward<S>1870</S> the south<S>1864</S>: and he measured<S>4058</S> the posts<S>352</S> thereof and the arches<S>361</S> thereof according to these measures<S>4060</S>.
26_40_24.ESV: And he led me toward the south, and behold, there was a gate on the south. And he measured its jambs and its vestibule; they had the same size as the others.
26_40_24.NASB: Then he led me toward the south, and behold, there was a gate toward the south; and he measured its side pillars and its porches according to those same measurements.
26_40_24.NLT: Then the man took me around to the south gateway and measured its various parts, and they were exactly the same as in the others.
26_40_24.WEB: He led me toward the south; and behold, a gate toward the south: and he measured its posts and its arches according to these measurements.
26_40_24.CSB17: He brought me to the south side, and there was also a gate on the south. He measured its jambs and portico; they had the same measurements as the others.
26_40_24.AMP: Then he led me toward the south, and behold, there was a gate toward the south; and he measured its side pillars and its porches, and they measured the same as the others.
26_40_24.MSG: Then he took me to the south side, to the south gate complex. He measured its gateposts and its porch. It was the same size as the others.
26_40_24.NIV:
The South Gate
Then he led me to the south side and I saw a gate facing south. He measured its jambs and its portico, and they had the same measurements as the others.
26_40_24.NRSVCE: Then he led me toward the south, and there was a gate on the south; and he measured its pilasters and its vestibule; they had the same dimensions as the others.

26_40_25.NKJV: There were windows in it and in its archways all around like those windows; its length was fifty cubits and its width twenty-five cubits.
26_40_25.KJV: And there were windows<S>2474</S> in it and in the arches<S>361</S> thereof round about<S>5439</S>, like those windows<S>2474</S>: the length<S>753</S> was fifty<S>2572</S> cubits<S>520</S>, and the breadth<S>7341</S> five<S>2568</S> and twenty<S>6242</S> cubits<S>520</S>.
26_40_25.ESV: Both it and its vestibule had windows all around, like the windows of the others. Its length was fifty cubits, and its breadth twenty-five cubits.
26_40_25.NASB: The gate and its porches had windows all around like those other windows; the length [was] fifty cubits and the width twenty-five cubits.
26_40_25.NLT: It had windows along the walls as the others did, and there was an entry room where the gateway passage opened into the outer courtyard. And like the others, the gateway passage was 87 1⁄2 feet long and 43 3⁄4 feet wide between the back walls of facing guard alcoves.
26_40_25.WEB: There were windows in it and in its arches all around, like those windows: the length was fifty cubits, and the width twenty-five cubits.
26_40_25.CSB17: Both the gate and its portico had windows all around, like the other windows. It was 87½ feet long and 43¾ feet wide.
26_40_25.AMP: The gate and its porches had windows all around like those windows [in the other gateways]; the length was fifty cubits and the width was twenty-five cubits.
26_40_25.MSG: The porch with its windows was the same size as those previously mentioned.
26_40_25.NIV: The gateway and its portico had narrow openings all around, like the openings of the others. It was fifty cubits long and twenty-five cubits wide.
26_40_25.NRSVCE: There were windows all around in it and in its vestibule, like the windows of the others; its depth was fifty cubits, and its width twenty-five cubits.

26_40_26.NKJV: Seven steps led up to it, and its archway was in front of them; and it had palm trees on its gateposts, one on this side and one on that side.
26_40_26.KJV: And there were seven<S>7651</S> steps<S>4609</S> to go up<S>5930</S> to it, and the arches<S>361</S> thereof were before<S>6440</S> them: and it had palm trees<S>8561</S>, one<S>259</S> on this side, and another<S>259</S> on that side, upon the posts<S>352</S> thereof.
26_40_26.ESV: And there were seven steps leading up to it, and its vestibule was before them, and it had palm trees on its jambs, one on either side.
26_40_26.NASB: [There were] seven steps going up to it, and its porches [were] in front of them; and it had palm tree ornaments on its side pillars, one on each side.
26_40_26.NLT: This gateway also had a stairway of seven steps leading up to it, and an entry room at the inner end, and palm tree decorations along the dividing walls.
26_40_26.WEB: There were seven steps to go up to it, and its arches were before them; and it had palm trees, one on this side, and another on that side, on its posts.
26_40_26.CSB17: Its stairway had seven steps, and its portico was ahead of them. It had palm trees on its jambs, one on each side.
26_40_26.AMP: There were seven steps going up to the gate, and its porches were in front of them; and it had palm tree decorations [carved] on its side pillars, one on each side.
26_40_26.MSG: It also had seven steps up to it. Its porch opened onto the outside courtyard, with palm trees decorating its gateposts on both sides.
26_40_26.NIV: Seven steps led up to it, with its portico opposite them; it had palm tree decorations on the faces of the projecting walls on each side.
26_40_26.NRSVCE: There were seven steps leading up to it; its vestibule was on the inside. It had palm trees on its pilasters, one on either side.

26_40_27.NKJV: There was also a gateway on the inner court, facing south; and he measured from gateway to gateway toward the south, one hundred cubits.
26_40_27.KJV: And there was a gate<S>8179</S> in the inner<S>6442</S> court<S>2691</S> toward<S>1870</S> the south<S>1864</S>: and he measured<S>4058</S> from gate<S>8179</S> to gate<S>8179</S> toward<S>1870</S> the south<S>1864</S> an hundred<S>3967</S> cubits<S>520</S>.
26_40_27.ESV: And there was a gate on the south of the inner court. And he measured from gate to gate toward the south, a hundred cubits.
26_40_27.NASB: The inner court had a gate toward the south; and he measured from gate to gate toward the south, a hundred cubits.
26_40_27.NLT: And here again, directly opposite the outer gateway, was another gateway that led into the inner courtyard. The distance between the two gateways was 175 feet.
26_40_27.WEB: There was a gate to the inner court toward the south: and he measured from gate to gate toward the south a hundred cubits.
26_40_27.CSB17: The inner court had a gate on the south. He measured from gate to gate on the south; it was 175 feet.
26_40_27.AMP: The inner courtyard had a gate toward the south; and he measured from gate to gate toward the south, a hundred cubits.
26_40_27.MSG: Opposite to it, the gate complex for the inner court faced south. He measured the distance across the courtyard from gate to gate: one hundred seventy-five feet.
26_40_27.NIV: The inner court also had a gate facing south, and he measured from this gate to the outer gate on the south side; it was a hundred cubits.

26_40_28.ESV: Then he brought me to the inner court through the south gate, and he measured the south gate. It was of the same size as the others.
26_40_28.NASB: Then he brought me to the inner court by the south gate; and he measured the south gate according to those same measurements.
26_40_28.NLT: Then the man took me to the south gateway leading into the inner courtyard. He measured it, and it had the same measurements as the other gateways.
26_40_28.WEB: Then he brought me to the inner court by the south gate: and he measured the south gate according to these measurements;
26_40_28.CSB17: Then he brought me to the inner court through the south gate. When he measured the south gate, it had the same measurements as the others.
26_40_28.AMP: Then the man (angel) brought me to the inner courtyard by the south gate; and he measured the south gate according to those same measurements.
26_40_28.MSG: He led me into the inside courtyard through the south gate complex. He measured it and found it the same as the outside ones.

Gates to the Inner Court

26_40_29.ESV: Its side rooms, its jambs, and its vestibule were of the same size as the others, and both it and its vestibule had windows all around. Its length was fifty cubits, and its breadth twenty-five cubits.

26_40_29.NLT: Its guard alcoves, dividing walls, and entry room were the same size as those in the others. It also had windows along its walls and in the entry room. And like the others, the gateway passage was 87 1⁄2 feet long and 43 3⁄4 feet wide.

26_40_29.CSB17: Its recesses, jambs, and portico had the same measurements as the others. Both it and its portico had windows all around. It was 87½ feet long and 43¾ feet wide.
26_40_29.AMP: Its guardrooms also, its side pillars and its porches measured the same as the others. And the gate and its porches had windows all around; the length was fifty cubits and the width was twenty-five cubits.

26_40_29.NIV: Its alcoves, its projecting walls and its portico had the same measurements as the others. The gateway and its portico had openings all around. It was fifty cubits long and twenty-five cubits wide.
26_40_29.NRSVCE: Its recesses, its pilasters, and its vestibule were of the same size as the others; and there were windows all around in it and in its vestibule; its depth was fifty cubits, and its width twenty-five cubits.

26_40_30.NKJV: There were archways all around, twenty-five cubits long and five cubits wide.
26_40_30.KJV: And the arches<S>361</S> round about<S>5439</S> were five<S>2568</S> and twenty<S>6242</S> cubits<S>520</S> long<S>753</S>, and five<S>2568</S> cubits<S>520</S> broad<S>7341</S>. <sup>broad: Heb. breadth</sup>
26_40_30.ESV: And there were vestibules all around, twenty-five cubits long and five cubits broad.
26_40_30.NASB: [There were] porches all around, twenty-five cubits long and five cubits wide.
26_40_30.NLT: (The entry rooms of the gateways leading into the inner courtyard were 14 feet across and 43 3⁄4 feet wide.)

26_40_31.NKJV: Its archways faced the outer court, palm trees were on its gateposts, and going up to it were eight steps.

26_40_49.WEB: The length of the porch was twenty cubits, and the width eleven cubits; even by the steps by which they went up to it: and there were pillars by the posts, one on this side, and another on that side.
26_40_49.CSB17: The portico was 35 feet <sup>[30]</sup> across and 21 <sup>[31]</sup> feet <sup>[32]</sup> deep, and 10 steps led <sup>[33]</sup> up to it. <sup>ⓚ</sup> There were pillars by the jambs, one on each side. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
26_40_49.AMP: The length of the porch was twenty cubits and the width eleven cubits; and at the stairway by which it was ascended, there were [two] columns beside the side pillars, one on each side [of the entrance].
26_40_49.MSG: The entrance to the gate complex was twenty-one feet wide and its connecting walls were four and a half feet thick. The vestibule itself was thirty-five feet wide and twenty-one feet deep. Ten steps led up to the porch. Columns flanked the gateposts.
26_40_49.NIV: The portico was twenty cubits wide, and twelve cubits from front to back. It was reached by a flight of stairs, and there were pillars on each side of the jambs.
26_40_49.NRSVCE: The depth of the vestibule was twenty cubits, and the width twelve cubits; ten steps led up to it; and there were pillars beside the pilasters on either side.

26_41_1.NKJV: Then he brought me into the sanctuary and measured the doorposts, six cubits wide on one side and six cubits wide on the other side—the width of the tabernacle.
26_41_1.KJV: Afterward he brought<S>935</S> me to the temple<S>1964</S>, and measured<S>4058</S> the posts<S>352</S>, six<S>8337</S> cubits<S>520</S> broad<S>7341</S> on the one side<S>6311</S>, and six<S>8337</S> cubits<S>520</S> broad<S>7341</S> on the other side<S>6311</S>, which was the breadth<S>7341</S> of the tabernacle<S>168</S>.
26_41_1.ESV: Then he brought me to the nave and measured the jambs. On each side six cubits  was the breadth of the jambs.
26_41_1.NASB: Then he brought me to the nave and measured the side pillars; six cubits wide on each side [was] the width of the side pillar.
26_41_1.NLT: After that, the man brought me into the sanctuary of the Temple. He measured the walls on either side of its doorway, and they were 10 1⁄2 feet thick.
26_41_1.WEB: He brought me to the temple, and measured the posts, six cubits wide on the one side, and six cubits wide on the other side, which was the width of the tent.
26_41_1.CSB17: Next he brought me into the great hall and measured the jambs; <sup>ⓜ</sup> on each side the width of the jamb was 10½ feet. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>[2]</sup>
26_41_1.AMP: Then he (the angel) brought me to the nave (outer sanctuary) and measured the side pillars; six cubits wide on each side was the width of the side pillar.
26_41_1.MSG: He brought me into the Temple itself and measured the doorposts on each side. Each was ten and a half feet thick.
26_41_1.NIV: Then the man brought me to the outer sanctuary and measured the jambs; the width of the jambs was six cubits on each side.
26_41_1.NRSVCE: Then he brought me to the nave, and measured the pilasters; on each side six cubits was the width of the pilasters.

26_41_2.NKJV: The width of the entryway was ten cubits, and the side walls of the entrance were five cubits on this side and five cubits on the other side; and he measured its length, forty cubits, and its width, twenty cubits.
26_41_2.KJV: And the breadth<S>7341</S> of the door<S>6607</S> was ten<S>6235</S> cubits<S>520</S>; and the sides<S>3802</S> of the door<S>6607</S> were five<S>2568</S> cubits<S>520</S> on the one side, and five<S>2568</S> cubits<S>520</S> on the other side: and he measured<S>4058</S> the length<S>753</S> thereof, forty<S>705</S> cubits<S>520</S>: and the breadth<S>7341</S>, twenty<S>6242</S> cubits<S>520</S>. <sup>door: or, entrance</sup>
26_41_2.ESV: And the breadth of the entrance was ten cubits, and the sidewalls of the entrance were five cubits on either side. And he measured the length of the nave,  forty cubits, and its breadth, twenty cubits.
26_41_2.NASB: The width of the entrance [was] ten cubits and the sides of the entrance [were] five cubits on each side. And he measured the length of the nave, forty cubits, and the width, twenty cubits.
26_41_2.NLT: The doorway was 17 1⁄2 feet wide, and the walls on each side of it were 8 3⁄4 feet long. The sanctuary itself was 70 feet long and 35 feet wide.
26_41_2.WEB: The width of the entrance was ten cubits;  and the sides of the entrance were five cubits on the one side, and five cubits on the other side: and he measured its length, forty cubits, and the width, twenty cubits.
26_41_2.CSB17: The width of the entrance was 17½ feet, <sup>[3]</sup> and the side walls of the entrance were 8¾ feet <sup>[4]</sup> wide on each side. He also measured the length of the great hall, 70 feet, <sup>[5]</sup> and the width, 35 feet. <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup>
26_41_2.AMP: The width of the entrance was ten cubits and the sides of the entrance were five cubits on each side; and he measured its length, forty cubits, and its width, twenty cubits.
26_41_2.MSG:
The entrance was seventeen and a half feet wide. The walls on each side were eight and three-quarters feet thick.
He also measured the Temple Sanctuary: seventy feet by thirty-five feet.
26_41_2.NIV: The entrance was ten cubits wide, and the projecting walls on each side of it were five cubits wide. He also measured the outer sanctuary; it was forty cubits long and twenty cubits wide.
26_41_2.NRSVCE: The width of the entrance was ten cubits; and the sidewalls of the entrance were five cubits on either side. He measured the length of the nave, forty cubits, and its width, twenty cubits.

26_41_3.NKJV: Also he went inside and measured the doorposts, two cubits; and the entrance, six cubits high; and the width of the entrance, seven cubits.
26_41_3.KJV: Then went<S>935</S> he inward<S>6441</S>, and measured<S>4058</S> the post<S>352</S> of the door<S>6607</S>, two<S>8147</S> cubits<S>520</S>; and the door<S>6607</S>, six<S>8337</S> cubits<S>520</S>; and the breadth<S>7341</S> of the door<S>6607</S>, seven<S>7651</S> cubits<S>520</S>.
26_41_3.ESV: Then he went into the inner room and measured the jambs of the entrance, two cubits; and the entrance, six cubits; and the sidewalls on either side  of the entrance, seven cubits.
26_41_3.NASB: Then he went inside and measured each side pillar of the doorway, two cubits, and the doorway, six cubits [high]; and the width of the doorway, seven cubits.
26_41_3.NLT: Then he went beyond the sanctuary into the inner room. He measured the walls on either side of its entrance, and they were 3 1⁄2 feet thick. The entrance was 10 1⁄2 feet wide, and the walls on each side of the entrance were 12 1⁄4 feet long.
26_41_3.WEB: Then went he inward, and measured each post of the entrance, two cubits; and the entrance, six cubits; and the width of the entrance, seven cubits.
26_41_3.CSB17: He went inside the next room and measured the jambs at the entrance; <sup>ⓐ</sup> they were 3½ feet <sup>[7]</sup> wide. The entrance was 10½ feet wide, and the width of the entrance’s side walls on each side <sup>[8]</sup> was 12¼ feet. <sup>[9]</sup>
26_41_3.AMP: Then he went inside [the inner sanctuary] and measured each side pillar of the doorway, two cubits, and the doorway, six cubits [high], and the width of the doorway, seven cubits.
26_41_3.MSG: He went further in and measured the doorposts at the entrance: Each was three and a half feet thick. The entrance itself was ten and a half feet wide, and the entrance walls were twelve and a quarter feet thick.
26_41_3.NIV: Then he went into the inner sanctuary and measured the jambs of the entrance; each was two cubits wide. The entrance was six cubits wide, and the projecting walls on each side of it were seven cubits wide.
26_41_3.NRSVCE: Then he went into the inner room and measured the pilasters of the entrance, two cubits; and the width of the entrance, six cubits; and the sidewalls of the entrance, seven cubits.

26_41_4.NKJV: He measured the length, twenty cubits; and the width, twenty cubits, beyond the sanctuary; and he said to me, “This is the Most Holy Place.”
26_41_4.KJV: So he measured<S>4058</S> the length<S>753</S> thereof, twenty<S>6242</S> cubits<S>520</S>; and the breadth<S>7341</S>, twenty<S>6242</S> cubits<S>520</S>, before<S>6440</S> the temple<S>1964</S>: and he said<S>559</S> unto me, This is the most<S>6944</S> holy<S>6944</S> place.
26_41_4.ESV: And he measured the length of the room, twenty cubits, and its breadth, twenty cubits, across the nave. And he said to me, “This is the Most Holy Place.”
26_41_4.NASB: He measured its length, twenty cubits, and the width, twenty cubits, before the nave; and he said to me, 'This is the most holy [place].'
26_41_4.NLT: The inner room of the sanctuary was 35 feet long and 35 feet wide. “This,” he told me, “is the Most Holy Place.”
26_41_4.WEB: He measured its length, twenty cubits, and the width, twenty cubits, before the temple: and he said to me, This is the most holy place.
26_41_4.CSB17: He then measured the length of the room adjacent to the great hall, 35 feet, and the width, 35 feet. <sup>ⓑ</sup> And he said to me, “This is the most holy place.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
26_41_4.AMP: He measured the length [of the interior of the inner sanctuary], twenty cubits, and the width, twenty cubits, opposite the nave (outer sanctuary); and he said to me, "This is the Most Holy Place."
26_41_4.MSG: He measured the inside Sanctuary, thirty-five feet square, set at the end of the main Sanctuary. He told me, "This is The Holy of Holies."
26_41_4.NIV: And he measured the length of the inner sanctuary; it was twenty cubits, and its width was twenty cubits across the end of the outer sanctuary. He said to me, “This is the Most Holy Place.”
26_41_4.NRSVCE: He measured the depth of the room, twenty cubits, and its width, twenty cubits, beyond the nave. And he said to me, This is the most holy place.

26_41_5.NKJV: Next, he measured the wall of the temple, six cubits. The width of each side chamber all around the temple was four cubits on every side.
26_41_5.KJV: After he measured<S>4058</S> the wall<S>7023</S> of the house<S>1004</S>, six<S>8337</S> cubits<S>520</S>; and the breadth<S>7341</S> of every side chamber<S>6763</S>, four<S>702</S> cubits<S>520</S>, round about<S>5439</S> the house<S>1004</S> on every side<S>5439</S>.
26_41_5.ESV: Then he measured the wall of the temple, six cubits thick, and the breadth of the side chambers, four cubits, all around the temple.
26_41_5.NASB: Then he measured the wall of the temple, six cubits; and the width of the side chambers, four cubits, all around about the house on every side.
26_41_5.NLT: Then he measured the wall of the Temple, and it was 10 1⁄2 feet thick. There was a row of rooms along the outside wall; each room was 7 feet wide.
26_41_5.WEB: Then he measured the wall of the house, six cubits; and the width of every side room, four cubits, all around the house on every side.
26_41_5.CSB17: Then he measured the wall of the temple; it was 10½ feet thick. The width of the side rooms all around the temple was 7 feet. <sup>[10]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>
26_41_5.AMP: Then he measured the wall of the temple, six cubits [thick, to accommodate side chambers]; and the width of every side chamber, four cubits, all around the temple on every side.
26_41_5.MSG: He measured the wall of the Temple. It was ten and a half feet thick. The side rooms around the Temple were seven feet wide.
26_41_5.NIV: Then he measured the wall of the temple; it was six cubits thick, and each side room around the temple was four cubits wide.
26_41_5.NRSVCE: Then he measured the wall of the temple, six cubits thick; and the width of the side chambers, four cubits, all around the temple.

26_41_6.NKJV: The side chambers were in three stories, one above the other, thirty chambers in each story; they rested on ledges which were for the side chambers all around, that they might be supported, but not fastened to the wall of the temple.
26_41_6.KJV: And the side<S>6763</S> chambers<S>6763</S> were three<S>7969</S>, one over another<S>6763</S>, and thirty<S>7970</S> in order<S>6471</S>; and they entered<S>935</S> into the wall<S>7023</S> which was of the house<S>1004</S> for the side chambers<S>6763</S> round about<S>5439</S>, that they might have hold<S>270</S>, but they had not hold<S>270</S> in the wall<S>7023</S> of the house<S>1004</S>. <sup>one: Heb. side chamber over side chamber</sup> <sup>thirty: or, three and thirty times, or, foot</sup> <sup>have hold: Heb. be holden</sup>
26_41_6.ESV: And the side chambers were in three stories, one over another, thirty in each story. There were offsets  all around the wall of the temple to serve as supports for the side chambers, so that they should not be supported by the wall of the temple.
26_41_6.NASB: The side chambers were in three stories, one above another, and thirty in each story; and the side chambers extended to the wall which [stood] on their inward side all around, that they might be fastened, and not be fastened into the wall of the temple [itself].
26_41_6.NLT: These side rooms were built in three levels, one above the other, with thirty rooms on each level. The supports for these side rooms rested on exterior ledges on the Temple wall; they did not extend into the wall.
26_41_6.WEB: The side rooms were in three stories, one over another, and thirty in order; and they entered into the wall which belonged to the house for the side rooms all around, that they might be supported, and not penetrate the wall of the house.
26_41_6.CSB17: The side rooms were arranged one above another in three stories of thirty rooms each. <sup>[11]</sup> There were ledges on the wall of the temple all around to serve as supports for the side rooms, so that the supports would not be in the temple wall itself. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
26_41_6.AMP: The side chambers were three stories [high], one above another, and thirty chambers in each story; and the side chambers extended to the wall which stood on their inward side all around, so that they would be attached, but not attached to the wall of the temple itself.
26_41_6.MSG: There were three floors of these side rooms, thirty rooms on each of the three floors. There were supporting beams around the Temple wall to hold up the side rooms, but they were freestanding, not attached to the wall itself.
26_41_6.NIV: The side rooms were on three levels, one above another, thirty on each level. There were ledges all around the wall of the temple to serve as supports for the side rooms, so that the supports were not inserted into the wall of the temple.
26_41_6.NRSVCE: The side chambers were in three stories, one over another, thirty in each story. There were offsets all around the wall of the temple to serve as supports for the side chambers, so that they should not be supported by the wall of the temple.

26_41_7.NKJV: As one went up from story to story, the side chambers became wider all around, because their supporting ledges in the wall of the temple ascended like steps; therefore the width of the structure increased as one went up from the lowest story to the highest by way of the middle one.
26_41_7.KJV: And there was an enlarging<S>7337</S>, and a winding about<S>5437</S> still upward<S>4605</S> to the side chambers<S>6763</S>: for the winding about<S>4141</S> of the house<S>1004</S> went still upward<S>4605</S> round about<S>5439</S> the house<S>1004</S>: therefore the breadth<S>7341</S> of the house<S>1004</S> was still upward<S>4605</S>, and so increased<S>5927</S> from the lowest<S>8481</S> chamber to the highest<S>5945</S> by the midst<S>8484</S>. <sup>there: Heb. it was made broader, and went round</sup>
26_41_7.ESV: And it became broader as it wound upward to the side chambers, because the temple was enclosed upward all around the temple. Thus the temple had a broad area upward, and so one went up from the lowest story to the top story through the middle story.
26_41_7.NASB: The side chambers surrounding the temple were wider at each successive story. Because the structure surrounding the temple went upward by stages on all sides of the temple, therefore the width of the temple [increased] as it went higher; and thus one went up from the lowest [story] to the highest by way of the second [story].
26_41_7.NLT: Each level was wider than the one below it, corresponding to the narrowing of the Temple wall as it rose higher. A stairway led up from the bottom level through the middle level to the top level.
26_41_7.WEB: The side rooms were wider on the higher levels, because the walls were narrower at the higher levels. Therefore the width of the house increased upward; and so one went up from the lowest level to the highest through the middle level.
26_41_7.CSB17: The side rooms surrounding the temple widened at each successive story, for the structure surrounding the temple went up by stages. This was the reason for the temple’s broadness as it rose. And so, one would go up from the lowest story to the highest by means of the middle one. <sup>[12]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup>
26_41_7.AMP: The side chambers became wider at each successive level as they encompassed the temple. Because the structure surrounding the temple went higher by stages on all sides of the temple, for that reason the width of the temple increased as it went higher; and thus one went up from the lowest story to the highest one by way of the second story.
26_41_7.MSG: The side rooms around the Temple became wider from first floor to second floor to third floor. A staircase went from the bottom floor, through the middle, and then to the top floor.
26_41_7.NIV: The side rooms all around the temple were wider at each successive level. The structure surrounding the temple was built in ascending stages, so that the rooms widened as one went upward. A stairway went up from the lowest floor to the top floor through the middle floor.
26_41_7.NRSVCE: The passageway of the side chambers widened from story to story; for the structure was supplied with a stairway all around the temple. For this reason the structure became wider from story to story. One ascended from the bottom story to the uppermost story by way of the middle one.

26_41_8.NKJV: I also saw an elevation all around the temple; it was the foundation of the side chambers, a full rod, that is, six cubits high.
26_41_8.KJV: I saw<S>7200</S> also the height<S>1363</S> of the house<S>1004</S> round about<S>5439</S>: the foundations<S>4328</S> of the side chambers<S>6763</S> were a full<S>4393</S> reed<S>7070</S> of six<S>8337</S> great<S>679</S> cubits<S>520</S>.
26_41_8.ESV: I saw also that the temple had a raised platform all around; the foundations of the side chambers measured a full reed of six long cubits.
26_41_8.NASB: I saw also that the house had a raised platform all around; the foundations of the side chambers were a full rod of six long cubits [in height].
26_41_8.NLT: I saw that the Temple was built on a terrace, which provided a foundation for the side rooms. This terrace was 10 1⁄2 feet high.
26_41_8.WEB: I saw also that the house had a raised base all around: the foundations of the side rooms were a full reed of six great cubits.
26_41_8.CSB17: I saw that the temple had a raised platform surrounding it; this foundation for the side rooms was 10½ feet high. <sup>[13]</sup> <sup>ⓖ</sup>
26_41_8.AMP: I also saw that the temple (house) had a raised platform all around it; the foundations of the side chambers measured a full rod of six long cubits in height.
26_41_8.MSG: I observed that the Temple had a ten-and-a-half-foot-thick raised base around it, which provided a foundation for the side rooms.
26_41_8.NIV: I saw that the temple had a raised base all around it, forming the foundation of the side rooms. It was the length of the rod, six long cubits.
26_41_8.NRSVCE: I saw also that the temple had a raised platform all around; the foundations of the side chambers measured a full reed of six long cubits.

26_41_9.NKJV: The thickness of the outer wall of the side chambers was five cubits, and so also the remaining terrace by the place of the side chambers of the temple.
26_41_9.KJV: The thickness<S>7341</S> of the wall<S>7023</S>, which was for the side chamber<S>6763</S> without<S>2351</S>, was five<S>2568</S> cubits<S>520</S>: and that which was left<S>3240</S> was the place<S>1004</S> of the side chambers<S>6763</S> that were within<S>1004</S>.
26_41_9.ESV: The thickness of the outer wall of the side chambers was five cubits. The free space between the side chambers of the temple and the
26_41_9.NASB: The thickness of the outer wall of the side chambers [was] five cubits. But the free space between the side chambers belonging to the temple
26_41_9.NLT: The outer wall of the Temple’s side rooms was 8 3⁄4 feet thick. This left an open area between these side rooms
26_41_9.WEB: The thickness of the wall, which was for the side rooms, on the outside, was five cubits: and that which was left was the place of the side rooms that belonged to the house.
26_41_9.CSB17: The thickness of the outer wall of the side rooms was 8¾ feet. The free space between the side rooms of the temple
26_41_9.AMP: The thickness of the outer wall of the side chambers was five cubits. But the free space between the side chambers belonging to the temple

26_41_9.NIV: The outer wall of the side rooms was five cubits thick. The open area between the side rooms of the temple
26_41_9.NRSVCE: The thickness of the outer wall of the side chambers was five cubits; and the free space between the side chambers of the temple

26_41_10.NKJV: And between it and the wall chambers was a width of twenty cubits all around the temple on every side.
26_41_10.KJV: And between the chambers<S>3957</S> was the wideness<S>7341</S> of twenty<S>6242</S> cubits<S>520</S> round about<S>5439</S> the house<S>1004</S> on every side<S>5439</S>.
26_41_10.ESV: other chambers was a breadth of twenty cubits all around the temple on every side.
26_41_10.NASB: and the [outer] chambers [was] twenty cubits in width all around the temple on every side.
26_41_10.NLT: and the row of rooms along the outer wall of the inner courtyard. This open area was 35 feet wide, and it went all the way around the Temple.
26_41_10.WEB: Between the rooms was a width of twenty cubits around the house on every side.
26_41_10.CSB17: and the outer chambers was 35 feet wide all around the temple. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
26_41_10.AMP: and the outer chambers was a width of twenty cubits all around the temple on every side.
26_41_10.MSG: and the priests' rooms was a thirty-five-foot-wide strip all around the Temple.
26_41_10.NIV: and the priests’ rooms was twenty cubits wide all around the temple.
26_41_10.NRSVCE: and the chambers of the court was a width of twenty cubits all around the temple on every side.

26_41_11.NKJV: The doors of the side chambers opened on the terrace, one door toward the north and another toward the south; and the width of the terrace was five cubits all around.
26_41_11.KJV: And the doors<S>6607</S> of the side chambers<S>6763</S> were toward the place that was left<S>3240</S>, one<S>259</S> door<S>6607</S> toward<S>1870</S> the north<S>6828</S>, and another<S>259</S> door<S>6607</S> toward the south<S>1864</S>: and the breadth<S>7341</S> of the place<S>4725</S> that was left<S>3240</S> was five<S>2568</S> cubits<S>520</S> round about<S>5439</S>.
26_41_11.ESV: And the doors of the side chambers opened on the free space, one door toward the north, and another door toward the south. And the breadth of the free space was five cubits all around.
26_41_11.NASB: The doorways of the side chambers toward the free space [consisted of] one doorway toward the north and another doorway toward the south; and the width of the free space [was] five cubits all around.
26_41_11.NLT: Two doors opened from the side rooms into the terrace yard, which was 8 3⁄4 feet wide. One door faced north and the other south.
26_41_11.WEB: The doors of the side rooms were toward an open area that was left, one door toward the north, and another door toward the south. The width of the open area was five cubits all around.
26_41_11.CSB17: The side rooms opened into the free space, one entrance toward the north and another to the south. The area of free space was 8¾ feet wide all around.
26_41_11.AMP: The doorways of the side chambers toward the free space were one doorway toward the north and another doorway toward the south; and the width of the free space was five cubits all around.
26_41_11.MSG: There were two entrances to the side rooms from the open area, one placed on the north side, the other on the south. There were eight and three-quarters feet of open space all around.
26_41_11.NIV: There were entrances to the side rooms from the open area, one on the north and another on the south; and the base adjoining the open area was five cubits wide all around.
26_41_11.NRSVCE: The side chambers opened onto the area left free, one door toward the north, and another door toward the south; and the width of the part that was left free was five cubits all around.

26_41_12.NKJV: The building that faced the separating courtyard at its western end was seventy cubits wide; the wall of the building was five cubits thick all around, and its length ninety cubits.
26_41_12.KJV: Now the building<S>1146</S> that was before<S>6440</S> the separate place<S>1508</S> at the end<S>6285</S> toward<S>1870</S> the west<S>3220</S> was seventy<S>7657</S> cubits<S>520</S> broad<S>7341</S>; and the wall<S>7023</S> of the building<S>1146</S> was five<S>2568</S> cubits<S>520</S> thick<S>7341</S> round about<S>5439</S>, and the length<S>753</S> thereof ninety<S>8673</S> cubits<S>520</S>.
26_41_12.ESV: The building that was facing the separate yard on the west side was seventy cubits broad, and the wall of the building was five cubits thick all around, and its length ninety cubits.

26_44_7.NKJV: When you brought in foreigners, uncircumcised in heart and uncircumcised in flesh, to be in My sanctuary to defile it—My house—and when you offered My food, the fat and the blood, then they broke My covenant because of all your abominations.

26_44_7.MSG: dragging irreverent and unrepentant outsiders, uncircumcised in heart and flesh, into my Sanctuary, feeding them the sacrificial offerings as if it were the food for a neighborhood picnic. With all your vile obscenities, you've broken trust with me, the solemn covenant I made with you.

26_44_13.NASB: 'And they shall not come near to Me to serve as a priest to Me, nor come near to any of My holy things, to the things that are most holy; but they will bear their shame and their abominations which they have committed.

26_44_15.MSG: "But the Levitical priests who descend from Zadok, who faithfully took care of my Sanctuary when everyone else went off and left me, are going to come into my presence and serve me. They are going to carry out the priestly work of offering the solemn sacrifices of worship. Decree of God, the Master.
26_44_15.NIV: “‘But the priests, who are Levites and descendants of Zadok and who faithfully carried out the duties of my sanctuary when the Israelites went astray from me, are to come near to minister before me; they are to stand before me to offer sacrifices of fat and blood, declares the Sovereign Lord.

26_44_17.NKJV: And it shall be, whenever they enter the gates of the inner court, that they shall put on linen garments; no wool shall come upon them while they minister within the gates of the inner court or within the house.

26_44_18.KJV: They shall have linen<S>6593</S> bonnets<S>6287</S> upon their heads<S>7218</S>, and shall have linen<S>6593</S> breeches<S>4370</S> upon their loins<S>4975</S>; they shall not gird<S>2296</S> themselves with any thing that causeth sweat<S>3154</S>. <sup>with: or, in sweating places: Heb. in, or, with sweat</sup>

26_44_19.NKJV: When they go out to the outer court, to the outer court to the people, they shall take off their garments in which they have ministered, leave them in the holy chambers, and put on other garments; and in their holy garments they shall not sanctify the people.

26_44_19.ESV: And when they go out into the outer court to the people, they shall put off the garments in which they have been ministering and lay them in the holy chambers. And they shall put on other garments, lest they transmit holiness to the people with their garments.
26_44_19.NASB: 'When they go out into the outer court, into the outer court to the people, they shall put off their garments in which they have been ministering and lay them in the holy chambers; then they shall put on other garments so that they will not transmit holiness to the people with their garments.

26_44_19.AMP: When they go out into the outer court, into the outer courtyard to the people, they shall take off the garments in which they have been ministering and leave them in the holy chambers; then they shall put on other clothing so that they will not transmit holiness to the people with their [ceremonial] garments.
26_44_19.MSG: When they go out into the outside courtyard where the people gather, they must first change out of the clothes they have been serving in, leaving them in the sacred rooms where they change to their everyday clothes, so that they don't trivialize their holy work by the way they dress.
26_44_19.NIV: When they go out into the outer court where the people are, they are to take off the clothes they have been ministering in and are to leave them in the sacred rooms, and put on other clothes, so that they do not consecrate the people by means of their garments.

26_44_20.NKJV: “They shall neither shave their heads nor let their hair grow long, but they shall keep their hair well trimmed.

26_44_22.NKJV: They shall not take as wife a widow or a divorced woman, but take virgins of the descendants of the house of Israel, or widows of priests.
26_44_22.KJV: Neither shall they take<S>3947</S> for their wives<S>802</S> a widow<S>490</S>, nor her that is put away<S>1644</S>: but they shall take<S>3947</S> maidens<S>1330</S> of the seed<S>2233</S> of the house<S>1004</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, or a widow<S>490</S> that had a priest<S>3548</S> before. <sup>put: Heb. thrust forth</sup> <sup>that had: Heb. from a priest</sup>

26_44_24.NKJV: In controversy they shall stand as judges, and judge it according to My judgments. They shall keep My laws and My statutes in all My appointed meetings, and they shall hallow My Sabbaths.

26_44_24.NASB: 'In a dispute they shall take their stand to judge; they shall judge it according to My ordinances. They shall also keep My laws and My statutes in all My appointed feasts and sanctify My sabbaths.
26_44_24.NLT: “They will serve as judges to resolve any disagreements among my people. Their decisions must be based on my regulations. And the priests themselves must obey my instructions and decrees at all the sacred festivals, and see to it that the Sabbaths are set apart as holy days.
26_44_24.WEB: In a controversy they shall stand to judge; according to my ordinances shall they judge it: and they shall keep my laws and my statutes in all my appointed feasts; and they shall make my Sabbaths holy.

26_44_24.MSG: "When there's a difference of opinion, the priests will arbitrate. They'll decide on the basis of my judgments, laws, and statutes. They are in charge of making sure the appointed feasts are honored and my Sabbaths kept holy in the ways I've commanded.

26_44_25.NKJV: “They shall not defile themselves by coming near a dead person. Only for father or mother, for son or daughter, for brother or unmarried sister may they defile themselves.

26_44_25.NIV: “‘A priest must not defile himself by going near a dead person; however, if the dead person was his father or mother, son or daughter, brother or unmarried sister, then he may defile himself.

26_44_28.NKJV: “It shall be, in regard to their inheritance, that I am their inheritance. You shall give them no possession in Israel, for I am their possession.
26_44_28.KJV: And it shall be unto them for an inheritance<S>5159</S>: I am their inheritance<S>5159</S>: and ye shall give<S>5414</S> them no possession<S>272</S> in Israel<S>3478</S>: I am their possession<S>272</S>.

26_44_28.NLT: “The priests will not have any property or possession of land, for I alone am their special possession.

26_44_29.NKJV: They shall eat the grain offering, the sin offering, and the trespass offering; every dedicated thing in Israel shall be theirs.

26_44_29.MSG: They'll take their meals from the grain offerings, the sin offerings, and the guilt offerings. Everything in Israel offered to God in worship is theirs.
26_44_29.NIV: They will eat the grain offerings, the sin offerings and the guilt offerings; and everything in Israel devoted to the Lord will belong to them.
26_44_29.NRSVCE: They shall eat the grain offering, the sin offering, and the guilt offering; and every devoted thing in Israel shall be theirs.

26_44_30.NKJV: The best of all firstfruits of any kind, and every sacrifice of any kind from all your sacrifices, shall be the priest’s; also you shall give to the priest the first of your ground meal, to cause a blessing to rest on your house.
26_44_30.KJV: And the first<S>7225</S> of all the firstfruits<S>1061</S> of all things, and every oblation<S>8641</S> of all, of every sort of your oblations<S>8641</S>, shall be the priest's<S>3548</S>: ye shall also give<S>5414</S> unto the priest<S>3548</S> the first<S>7225</S> of your dough<S>6182</S>, that he may cause the blessing<S>1293</S> to rest<S>5117</S> in thine house<S>1004</S>. <sup>And the first: or, And the chief</sup>

26_44_30.NASB: 'The first of all the first fruits of every kind and every contribution of every kind, from all your contributions, shall be for the priests; you shall also give to the priest the first of your dough to cause a blessing to rest on your house.
26_44_30.NLT: The first of the ripe fruits and all the gifts brought to the LORD will go to the priests. The first batch of dough must also be given to the priests so the LORD will bless your homes.

You shall also give to the priest the first of your coarse meal and bread dough, so that a blessing may rest on your house.
26_44_30.MSG: The best of everything grown, plus all special gifts, comes to the priests. All that is given in worship to God goes to them. Serve them first. Serve from your best and your home will be blessed.
26_44_30.NIV: The best of all the firstfruits and of all your special gifts will belong to the priests. You are to give them the first portion of your ground meal so that a blessing may rest on your household.
26_44_30.NRSVCE: The first of all the first fruits of all kinds, and every offering of all kinds from all your offerings, shall belong to the priests; you shall also give to the priests the first of your dough, in order that a blessing may rest on your house.

26_44_31.NKJV: The priests shall not eat anything, bird or beast, that died naturally or was torn by wild beasts.

26_45_9.NKJV: ‘Thus says the Lord GOD: “Enough, O princes of Israel! Remove violence and plundering, execute justice and righteousness, and stop dispossessing My people,” says the Lord GOD.
26_45_9.KJV: Thus saith<S>5002</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>; Let it suffice<S>7227</S> you, O princes<S>5387</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>: remove<S>5493</S> violence<S>2555</S> and spoil<S>7701</S>, and execute<S>6213</S> judgment<S>4941</S> and justice<S>6666</S>, take away<S>7311</S> your exactions<S>1646</S> from my people<S>5971</S>, saith<S>559</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>. <sup>exactions: Heb. expulsions</sup>
26_45_9.ESV: “Thus says the Lord GOD: Enough, O princes of Israel! Put away violence and oppression, and execute justice and righteousness. Cease your evictions of my people, declares the Lord GOD.
26_45_9.NASB: 'Thus says the Lord GOD, 'Enough, you princes of Israel; put away violence and destruction, and practice justice and righteousness. Stop your expropriations from My people,' declares the Lord GOD.
26_45_9.NLT: “For this is what the Sovereign LORD says: Enough, you princes of Israel! Stop your violence and oppression and do what is just and right. Quit robbing and cheating my people out of their land. Stop expelling them from their homes, says the Sovereign LORD.
26_45_9.WEB: Thus says the Lord Yahweh: Let it suffice you, princes of Israel: remove violence and plunder, and execute justice and righteousness; dispossessing my people, says the Lord Yahweh.
26_45_9.CSB17: “This is what the Lord GOD says: You have gone too far, <sup>[11]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> princes of Israel! Put away violence and oppression and do what is just and right. <sup>ⓕ</sup> Put an end to your evictions of my people.” This is the declaration of the Lord GOD.
26_45_9.AMP: 'Thus says the Lord GOD, "Enough, O princes of Israel! Put away violence and destruction, and practice justice and righteousness. Stop your forceful seizure of property from My people," says the Lord GOD.
26_45_9.MSG: "This is the Message of God, the Master: 'I've put up with you long enough, princes of Israel! Quit bullying and taking advantage of my people. Do what's just and right for a change.
26_45_9.NIV: “‘This is what the Sovereign Lord says: You have gone far enough, O princes of Israel! Give up your violence and oppression and do what is just and right. Stop dispossessing my people, declares the Sovereign Lord.
26_45_9.NRSVCE: Thus says the Lord GOD: Enough, O princes of Israel! Put away violence and oppression, and do what is just and right. Cease your evictions of my people, says the Lord GOD.

26_45_10.NKJV: “You shall have honest scales, an honest ephah, and an honest bath.

26_45_10.ESV: “You shall have just balances, a just ephah, and a just bath.
26_45_10.NASB: 'You shall have just balances, a just ephah and a just bath.
26_45_10.NLT: Use only honest weights and scales and honest measures, both dry and liquid.
26_45_10.WEB: You shall have just balances, and a just ephah,  and a just bath.
